title: Cubica State Fixture
description: Pinned named snapshot of runtime game state that becomes a reviewable authoring artifact under games/<gameId>/authoring/fixtures/<fixtureId>.json (ADR-057 §4.9). A fixture provides the state context for the editor Design mode and a starting point for a playthrough. Runtime-api, player-web and the compiler never read fixture files; the editor passes the captured state into a preview-only restore endpoint.

id.description: Stable ASCII-first identifier of the fixture, used for the file name and references. Same identifier shape as authoring entity ids.

_label.description: Human-readable editor name for the fixture. Cyrillic labels are allowed and expected for Russian authoring.

screenRef.description: Optional id of the UI screen this fixture is bound to. Must match a screen id in the active preview channel UI manifest. A fixture may be bound to a step only, so this field is optional.

stepRef.description: Optional id of the game chronology step this fixture is bound to. Must match a step id in the game manifest chronology. A fixture may be bound to a screen only, so this field is optional.

state.description: Full snapshot of the runtime game state (stage, metrics, objects) captured at pin time. The shape is intentionally open because it mirrors whatever runtime state the game produces.

manifestHash.description: Deterministic content hash of the authoring manifests at the moment the fixture was captured. When it no longer matches the current manifests the editor raises the fixture-stale diagnostic. Format: sha256-<64 lowercase hex chars>.

sourceTraceRef.description: Optional informational pointer to the source playthrough trace the fixture was captured from, for example .tmp/editor-playthroughs/<traceId>#<seq>. It targets the garbage-collected .tmp area, so a stale reference is allowed and is not an error.

note.description: Optional free-form author note explaining why this fixture exists.
